note: Costs shown are for California resident undergraduate students, on-campus housing. Non-residents pay ~$31,026 additional in supplemental tuition.

systemwideTuition.note: UC systemwide tuition is the same at all 9 campuses

University of California, Berkeley.notes: One of the higher-cost UC campuses due to Bay Area cost of living

University of California, Los Angeles.notes: LA cost of living is high; off-campus housing in Westwood is expensive

University of California, San Diego.notes: La Jolla has a moderate cost of living compared to other coastal UC locations

University of California, Santa Barbara.notes: Isla Vista (adjacent to campus) has affordable student housing options

University of California, Davis.notes: Davis is a college town with relatively affordable off-campus housing

University of California, Irvine.notes: Orange County cost of living is moderate; campus has extensive on-campus housing

University of California, Riverside.notes: Most affordable UC campus; Inland Empire has lower cost of living

University of California, Santa Cruz.notes: Santa Cruz housing is expensive; many students live in college residential buildings

University of California, Merced.notes: Least expensive UC campus; Central Valley has very low cost of living. Highest financial aid rates in the UC system.

financialAidContext.ucBlueAndGold: California residents with family income under ~$80,000/year qualify for UC Blue and Gold, which covers tuition and fees (~$14,000/year) when combined with Cal Grant.
financialAidContext.calGrant: Cal Grant A covers up to $9,220/year in tuition for eligible CA residents (file FAFSA by March 2).
financialAidContext.netCostNote: Many students pay significantly less than sticker price. UC Merced and UC Riverside have the highest % of students receiving aid.
financialAidContext.totalDebtNote: Average UC transfer student graduates with ~$14,000-22,000 in debt (lower than national average for 4-year schools).
